# UC-13.1.7 · Splunk KV Store Health, Replication Lag, Storage Engine Performance, and ES/ITSI Lookup Dependency Detection

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Reliability, Availability &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch the small database that keeps apps, lookups, and investigations in sync across cluster members. When it falls behind, runs out of breathing room, or cannot copy changes cleanly, we raise a clear signal before screens go blank and jobs that write tables start piling up.*

---

## Description

Detects Splunk KV Store degradation on MongoDB WiredTiger by correlating introspection engine metrics, mongod log diagnostics, REST replication_status and replSetMembers snapshots, oplog_lag_seconds between search head cluster members, connection pool pressure, eviction and dirty cache stress, and rapid db_size_bytes growth on security and service intelligence collections so correlation searches, KPI lookups, dashboards, and outputlookup writers fail visibly before silent data gaps spread.

## Value

Platform and application owners shorten mean time to innocence when premium dashboards empty out or scheduled writes stall: this UC fuses introspection, mongo telemetry, REST status, collection sizing, and inventory criticality into one severity-ranked row per cluster and collection so teams fix the KV tier—replication, cache, disk, or runaway collection growth—before incident responders blame rule logic or network paths that are actually healthy.

## Implementation

Schedule platform_uc_13_1_7_kvstore_health_replication_wiredtiger every five minutes from the Monitoring Console with earliest=-4h@h, maintain splunk_kvstore_collection_inventory.csv for app and owner context, tune cache_hit_ratio oplog eviction and growth thresholds per cluster class, route page rows to platform KV on-call with MC drilldowns, prefer indexed REST replicas where inline rest is disallowed, and archive weekly alert exports to a restricted evidence index.

## Evidence

Saved search platform_uc_13_1_7_kvstore_health_replication_wiredtiger with five-minute schedule; Monitoring Console KV Store dashboard drilldowns; splunk_kvstore_collection_inventory.csv version hash archived with change tickets; weekly CSV exports of alert results to a restricted platform_evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab search head cluster under change control, pause KV replication briefly or stress WiredTiger cache with vendor-permitted load, confirm platform_uc_13_1_7_kvstore_health_replication_wiredtiger returns page or warn with non-null recommended_action within ten minutes while inventory joins populate.

### Negative scenario

After recovery when Monitoring Console KV panels show healthy replication, oplog_lag_seconds decay below warn thresholds, cache_hit_ratio stabilizes, and growth_bytes_per_hr returns to baseline, confirm severities return to info and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to page and warn only.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the Splunk platform team that operates search head clusters with Mongo-backed application storage, the incident response engineers who depend on security app lookups, and the service intelligence owners whose KPI modules read collections on every drilldown. This use case isolates Splunk KV Store health on the MongoDB storage engine that backs collections, replication between search head cluster members, WiredTiger cache and eviction behavior, connection pressure from correlation and enrichment workloads, and the footprint of collections such as notable_audit, incident_review, and service_kpi that grow when investigations never age out. The narrative complements rather than replaces sibling gold cards: UC-13.1.10 remains authoritative for captain elections, deployer bundle replication, and generic search head clustering symptoms without the WiredTiger and collection-level drill path prescribed here. UC-13.1.11 remains indexer cluster bucket replication and fixup math, not Mongo oplog lag between SHC peers. UC-13.1.15 remains certificate inventory and TLS chain trust for KV member listeners rather than runtime cache hit ratio or eviction storms. UC-13.1.1 remains ingestion queue saturation on indexers rather than application-tier Mongo pressure on search heads. Splunk Enterprise Security correlation searches, risk modifiers, and many lookups lean on timely KV reads; Splunk ITSI service KPI base searches and entity import jobs lean on collections for state and thresholds. When replication_status degrades or primaries flap, those reads and writes fail open, stall, or return empty sets, which looks like mysteriously quiet dashboards rather than a database outage until you open the Monitoring Console KV Store dashboard or inspect mongo logs.

Scope the deployment around hosts that actually run KV: typically search head cluster members and standalone search heads where server.conf enables the service, not universal forwarders at the edge. RBAC for authors needs read access to index=_introspection with sourcetype=kvstore, index=_internal with sourcetype=mongod where your build emits Mongo diagnostics into splunkd indexing paths, and permission to execute rest /services/kvstore/status from a hardened management search head or the Monitoring Console search tier. Distributed search must reach every KV-capable peer so introspection rows are not hollow for silent members. Legal reviewers should confirm collection names and application identifiers in splunk_kvstore_collection_inventory.csv are acceptable in shared indexes because they reveal which premium modules you operate.

Field normalization expectations: cache_hit_ratio may appear under vendor-specific keys in introspection JSON; dirty_bytes and eviction_pressure may surface as WiredTiger cache and walk counters in mongod structured logs; connections_active tracks client sessions including scheduled searches that hold cursors; oplog_lag_seconds estimates how far secondaries trail the primary for configuration and collection writes; replSetMembers captures member state text or JSON fragments from REST or logs; kvstore_collection names the logical collection or namespace; db_size_bytes estimates on-disk or reported size for capacity governance. Tag cluster consistently with your search head cluster label so joins align with CMDB and inventory lookups even when host is a containerized short name.

Risk briefing: rolling KV restarts during maintenance legitimately pause replication and elevate oplog lag for minutes; pair alerts with change_ticket_id metadata and captain stability checks from UC-13.1.10 before paging application owners. Bulk backup jobs and resync operations inflate collection sizes and IOPS without implying an attack; compare growth_bytes_per_hr to known backup windows. Development clusters that replay production bundles can inherit huge collections overnight; inventory criticality should downgrade non-production rows. Executive dashboards that only glance at replication_status miss eviction pressure that still starves interactive lookups; keep the introspection and mongod lanes attached.

Differentiation recap: Mongo-backed KV Store replication, WiredTiger performance, collection growth, and premium-app lookup dependency signals—not generic SHC captain churn alone, not indexer bucket replication, not certificate expiry tables, not ingestion queue fill ratios.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up four complementary lanes the multisearch arms consume plus inventory and Monitoring Console context.

Lane A, introspection engine metrics: confirm index=_introspection ingests sourcetype=kvstore snapshots from every KV-enabled host on a five-minute cadence or finer during incidents. Validate fields that map to cache_hit_ratio, dirty_bytes, eviction_pressure, and connections_active appear when you stress a lab host with lookup-heavy searches. If introspection is disabled by policy, re-enable the minimal KV subset or mirror the Monitoring Console forwarded feeds.

Lane B, mongod diagnostics: ensure index=_internal or your approved security index captures sourcetype=mongod lines emitted by Splunk’s embedded Mongo where log-to-splunkd is configured. TRUNCATE and LINE_BREAKER must allow long WiredTiger lines. When mongod lands only on local disk, forward it through the same heavy forwarder path you use for other _internal subsets.

Lane C, REST replication snapshots: schedule a short saved search or external poller that executes rest /services/kvstore/status per member and indexes JSON results into a restricted index such as index=platform_rest with sourcetype=kvstore_rest_status if your security team forbids inline rest in scheduled searches. The SPL arm uses inline rest for interactive readiness; production schedules should prefer indexed replicas to avoid distributed search fan-out surprises.

Lane D, collection sizing: keep introspection rows that include per-collection db_size_bytes or equivalent; where absent, supplement with scripted dumps from show collections style tooling that you are allowed to run. Highlight notable_audit, incident_review, service_kpi, and custom lookup backing collections in splunk_kvstore_collection_inventory.csv with columns kvstore_collection, app_context, criticality, owner_hint, and es_itsi_signal so severity can reflect business criticality.

Lookup hygiene: refresh splunk_kvstore_collection_inventory.csv weekly or automate a KV Store collection inventory job that outputs the same schema. Include owner_hint links to runbooks for trimming aging notables, rebuilding incident review history under change control, and ITSI summary maintenance.

Monitoring Console posture: enable the KV Store dashboard cards, verify replication_status colors match REST ground truth on a canary host, and confirm distributed mode sees every SHC member. props.conf on forwarders carrying _introspection should preserve TIMESTAMP_FIELDS so lag math aligns with mongo log timestamps.

Example operator snippets for design reviews:

```bash
curl -k -u admin:changeme https://localhost:8089/services/kvstore/status
splunkd introspection listings for kvstore on the instance under $SPLUNK_HOME/var/log/splunk
```

Pre-save validation: index=_introspection sourcetype=kvstore earliest=-1h must return rows on a KV-enabled lab host; sourcetype=mongod should move when you intentionally run a heavy outputlookup test under change control; rest /services/kvstore/status must return readable replication fields from the same host.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as platform_uc_13_1_7_kvstore_health_replication_wiredtiger with a five-minute schedule in production, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and throttle duplicate cluster,kvstore_collection rows for twenty minutes unless severity escalates from warn to page. Route page severities to the platform KV on-call with Monitoring Console deep links; route warn severities to a chat channel with Job Inspector links and the recommended_action token.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans four arms—introspection engine metrics, internal mongod diagnostics, inline REST mongo_replset_status snapshot, and introspection-backed collection sizing—so a silent single lane cannot hide a real outage. coalesce() ladders absorb field naming drift across Splunk minor versions and exporter flattening choices. streamstats estimates hour-normalized growth_bytes_per_hr for large collections without expensive transaction commands. eventstats adds cluster_peak_oplog and cluster_sensitive_coll_peak for fleet context on dashboards. case() maps oplog lag, replication text, cache hit collapse, eviction pressure, connection storms, collection growth, and degraded replication verbs into page versus warn versus info tiers. join enriches business context from splunk_kvstore_collection_inventory.csv and adds kv_introspection_evt_volume from a tstats volume proxy keyed on rep_host so sudden drops in telemetry volume surface as investigation hints alongside numeric thresholds.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.7 Splunk KV Store health: fuse _introspection sourcetype=kvstore engine and collection sizing, _internal sourcetype=mongod WiredTiger style signals, REST /services/kvstore/status replication snapshot, and join inventory plus tstats volume proxy. Tunables: earliest=-4h@h cache_hit_warn=0.82 oplog_page_sec=120 oplog_warn_sec=45 eviction_page=8000 conn_warn=7000 growth_warn_gib_per_hr=4")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_introspection sourcetype=kvstore earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,shcluster_label,shc_id,"standalone"))))
      | eval cache_hit_ratio=tonumber(coalesce(cache_hit_ratio,wiredTiger_cache_hit_ratio,cache_hit_pct,"0"))
      | eval dirty_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(dirty_bytes,wt_dirty_bytes,bytes_dirty,"0"))
      | eval eviction_pressure=tonumber(coalesce(eviction_pressure,wt_eviction_pressure,evictions_per_sec,"0"))
      | eval connections_active=tonumber(coalesce(connections_active,current_connections,mongo_connections_current,"0"))
      | eval oplog_lag_seconds=tonumber(coalesce(oplog_lag_seconds,repl_lag_sec,replication_lag_sec,"0"))
      | eval replSetMembers=coalesce(replSetMembers,member_health_summary,replication_member_state,"")
      | eval kvstore_collection=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(collection,kvstore_collection,table,metric_scope,"engine_rollups"))))
      | eval db_size_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(db_size_bytes,size_bytes,storageSize,dataSize,"0"))
      | eval replication_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(replication_status,kv_replication_status,"intro_lane"))))
      | eval lane="introspection_kvstore_engine"
      | fields _time host cluster lane cache_hit_ratio dirty_bytes eviction_pressure connections_active oplog_lag_seconds replSetMembers kvstore_collection db_size_bytes replication_status ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=mongod earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,shcluster_label,shc_id,"standalone"))))
      | eval cache_hit_ratio=tonumber(coalesce(cache_hit_ratio,cache_ratio,"0"))
      | eval dirty_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(dirty_bytes,bytesCurrentlyInTheCacheDirty,tracked_dirty_bytes,"0"))
      | eval eviction_pressure=tonumber(coalesce(eviction_pressure,eviction_server_walksPerSec,walks_per_sec,"0"))
      | eval connections_active=tonumber(coalesce(connections_active,connections_current,conn_current,"0"))
      | eval oplog_lag_seconds=tonumber(coalesce(oplog_lag_seconds,optime_lag_seconds,secondary_lag_seconds,"0"))
      | eval replSetMembers=coalesce(replSetMembers,repl_member_state,memberState,"mongod_log")
      | eval kvstore_collection="mongod_diag"
      | eval db_size_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(db_size_bytes,dataSizeBytes,"0"))
      | eval replication_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(replication_status,"mongod_internal"))))
      | eval lane="internal_mongod"
      | fields _time host cluster lane cache_hit_ratio dirty_bytes eviction_pressure connections_active oplog_lag_seconds replSetMembers kvstore_collection db_size_bytes replication_status ]
    [ | rest splunk_server=local /services/kvstore/status count=0
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,serverName,servername,"rest_local"))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(shcluster_label,shc_id,"rest_status"))))
      | eval replication_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(replication_status,status,title,"rest_unknown"))))
      | eval replSetMembers=coalesce(replSetMembers,members,member_status,content_replSetMembers,"rest_members_unparsed")
      | eval oplog_lag_seconds=tonumber(coalesce(oplog_lag_seconds,optimeLag,optime_lag_seconds,"0"))
      | eval cache_hit_ratio=0
      | eval dirty_bytes=0
      | eval eviction_pressure=0
      | eval connections_active=0
      | eval kvstore_collection="mongo_replset_status"
      | eval db_size_bytes=0
      | eval lane="rest_kvstore_status"
      | fields _time host cluster lane replication_status replSetMembers oplog_lag_seconds cache_hit_ratio dirty_bytes eviction_pressure connections_active kvstore_collection db_size_bytes ]
    [ search index=_introspection sourcetype=kvstore earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,shcluster_label,shc_id,"standalone"))))
      | eval kvstore_collection=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(collection,kvstore_collection,app_collection,ns_collection,"unscoped_collection"))))
      | eval db_size_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(db_size_bytes,size_bytes,storageSize,allocated_bytes,"0"))
      | where db_size_bytes>1048576 OR match(kvstore_collection,"notable_audit|incident_review|service_kpi|itsi|lookup")
      | eval cache_hit_ratio=0
      | eval dirty_bytes=0
      | eval eviction_pressure=0
      | eval connections_active=0
      | eval oplog_lag_seconds=0
      | eval replSetMembers="size_only"
      | eval replication_status="collection_metrics"
      | eval lane="kvstore_collection_sizes"
      | fields _time host cluster lane kvstore_collection db_size_bytes cache_hit_ratio dirty_bytes eviction_pressure connections_active oplog_lag_seconds replSetMembers replication_status ]
| eval cluster=coalesce(cluster,"unknown_cluster")
| eval host=coalesce(host,"unknown_host")
| eval kvstore_collection=coalesce(kvstore_collection,"unspecified_kv_collection")
| sort 0 + cluster + host + kvstore_collection - _time
| streamstats window=8 current=t global=f last(db_size_bytes) AS prev_db_bytes last(_time) AS prev_ts BY cluster host kvstore_collection
| eval growth_bytes_per_hr=if(isnotnull(prev_db_bytes) AND isnotnull(prev_ts) AND (_time-prev_ts)>120, round((db_size_bytes-prev_db_bytes)/((_time-prev_ts)/3600.0),4), null())
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="rest_kvstore_status",oplog_lag_seconds,null()))) AS cluster_peak_oplog max(eval(if(match(kvstore_collection,"notable_audit|incident_review|service_kpi"),db_size_bytes,null()))) AS cluster_sensitive_coll_peak BY cluster
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen
    max(cache_hit_ratio) AS cache_hit_ratio
    max(dirty_bytes) AS dirty_bytes
    max(eviction_pressure) AS eviction_pressure
    max(connections_active) AS connections_active
    max(oplog_lag_seconds) AS oplog_lag_seconds
    max(db_size_bytes) AS db_size_bytes
    max(growth_bytes_per_hr) AS growth_bytes_per_hr
    values(replSetMembers) AS replSetMembers
    values(lane) AS lane_vals
    values(replication_status) AS repl_status_vals
    first(host) AS rep_host
  BY cluster kvstore_collection
| eval coalesce_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lane_vals),0)
| eval repl_status=mvindex(mvdedup(repl_status_vals),0)
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(oplog_lag_seconds,0)>=120 OR match(lower(mvjoin(replSetMembers," ")),"down|rollback|unknown|invalid"),"page",
    coalesce(oplog_lag_seconds,0)>=45,"warn",
    coalesce(cache_hit_ratio,0)>0 AND coalesce(cache_hit_ratio,1)<0.82 AND coalesce(coalesce_lane,"x")=="introspection_kvstore_engine","warn",
    coalesce(eviction_pressure,0)>=8000,"page",
    coalesce(connections_active,0)>=7000,"warn",
    coalesce(growth_bytes_per_hr,0)>=4294967296,"warn",
    match(lower(coalesce(repl_status,"")),"degrad|recover|initialsync|rollback"),"page",
    true(),"info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    coalesce(oplog_lag_seconds,0)>=45,"validate_shc_kv_primary_secondary_order_and_disk_latency_then_mc_kv_dashboard",
    coalesce(cache_hit_ratio,0)>0 AND coalesce(cache_hit_ratio,1)<0.82,"increase_wiredtiger_cache_or_reduce_kv_write_load_and_disk_contention",
    coalesce(eviction_pressure,0)>=8000,"relieve_wiredtiger_eviction_pressure_scale_disk_or_reduce_bulk_outputlookup",
    coalesce(connections_active,0)>=7000,"find_connection_storms_from_correlation_searches_and_stuck_scheduled_lookups",
    coalesce(growth_bytes_per_hr,0)>=4294967296,"trim_notable_audit_incident_review_service_kpi_collections_after_backup",
    true(),"refresh_kvstore_status_rest_and_compare_mc_replication_panels")
| join type=left max=0 kvstore_collection
    [| inputlookup splunk_kvstore_collection_inventory.csv
     | eval kvstore_collection=lower(trim(toString(kvstore_collection)))
     | fields kvstore_collection app_context criticality owner_hint es_itsi_signal ]
| join type=left max=0 rep_host
    [| tstats count WHERE index=_introspection sourcetype=kvstore earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY splunk_server
     | eval rep_host=lower(trim(toString(splunk_server)))
     | stats sum(count) AS kv_introspection_evt_volume BY rep_host ]
| fillnull value=0 kv_introspection_evt_volume
| eval app_context=coalesce(app_context,"unmapped_collection")
| eval criticality=coalesce(criticality,"medium")
| eval owner_hint=coalesce(owner_hint,"platform_kv_ops")
| eval es_itsi_signal=coalesce(es_itsi_signal,"none")
| table cluster rep_host kvstore_collection cache_hit_ratio dirty_bytes eviction_pressure connections_active oplog_lag_seconds replSetMembers db_size_bytes growth_bytes_per_hr severity recommended_action last_seen coalesce_lane repl_status app_context criticality kv_introspection_evt_volume es_itsi_signal owner_hint
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[platform_uc_13_1_7_kvstore_health_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_1_7_kvstore_health_replication_wiredtiger | where severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance notes: if Job Inspector shows multisearch skew, materialize each lane hourly into a summary index keyed on cluster and kvstore_collection, widen operational alerts to earliest=-2h@h on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. Cap rest fan-out by preferring indexed REST replicas at scale.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on a KV-capable host. Compare rest /services/kvstore/status replication_status and member hints to the latest rest arm row in Splunk within one polling interval. When they disagree, verify clock skew, duplicate host keys, and forwarder filters before blaming parsers.

Open the Monitoring Console KV Store dashboard and align colors to severity in this search for the same cluster label. Drill into sourcetype=kvstore introspection for cache_hit_ratio and dirty_bytes trends during a controlled load test.

Validate mongod lane signals against mongo log files on disk for WiredTiger eviction messages and connection warnings. Confirm oplog_lag_seconds directionally matches vendor guidance for your maintenance window.

Collection sizing: pick notable_audit or incident_review rows, compare db_size_bytes to exporter tooling or backup manifests, and ensure growth_bytes_per_hr spikes only when expected jobs run.

Synthetic positive test: under change control on a lab member, induce a brief replication pause or cache pressure scenario vendor docs permit, confirm severity rises with non-null recommended_action, then recover and confirm return to info.

Negative test: on a healthy cluster after maintenance completes, confirm filtering to page and warn returns zero rows for fifteen consecutive windows.

Job Inspector: confirm scanned volumes remain proportional to introspection cardinality; unexpected explosion suggests unbounded wildcards.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Primary flaps while secondaries show rollback text in replSetMembers: stabilize captain and deployer activity per search head cluster runbooks, verify disk latency and filesystem fullness on the KV primary, and only then restart members in vendor-supported order with Maintenance Mode annotations.

Case 2 — oplog_lag_seconds climbs during bundle pushes: pause nonessential outputlookup jobs, verify deployer replication finished, and watch lag decay before clearing alerts; correlate with UC-13.1.10 bundle timelines without merging the saved searches.

Case 3 — cache_hit_ratio collapses while dirty_bytes grows: increase WiredTiger cache within supported bounds, move hot volumes to faster disk, and reduce concurrent bulk writes from correlation searches writing the same collection.

Case 4 — eviction_pressure spikes with user-visible lookup timeouts: identify heavy transforms and scheduled searches touching the same collection, spread schedules, and validate antivirus or storage snapshots are not freezing Mongo files.

Case 5 — connections_active nears limits with stuck searches: use search job inspector on the search head to find hung outputlookup or inputlookup loops, cancel runaway jobs, and tune max concurrent scheduled searches per app.

Case 6 — notable_audit or incident_review db_size_bytes grows without retention policy: execute approved trimming or archival, rebuild collections only after backup, and document evidence for auditors.

Case 7 — service_kpi collections balloon after ITSI imports: coordinate with service owners to delete stale KPI snapshots under change control and validate ITSI maintenance windows.

Case 8 — REST arm empty while introspection flows: repair distributed search connectivity to the target member, fix token permissions for rest /services/kvstore/status, or enable the indexed REST poller lane.

Case 9 — False severity after version upgrade field rename: update coalesce ladders in the SPL, refresh knowledge bundle on search heads, and rerun validation on a canary before fleet rollout.

Case 10 — Enterprise Security correlation searches return zero rows while KV shows degraded replication: prioritize KV recovery before debugging rule logic; rules may be healthy but data is unreadable.

Case 11 — ITSI glass tables show gaps while KPI searches succeed interactively: confirm KV collections backing imports are replicated, not just CSV lookups, and verify entity import schedules are not stalled on writes.

Case 12 — Fleet-wide healthy KV: weekly spot-check Splunk rows against Monitoring Console and REST on every production search head cluster to confirm parsers, inventory lookups, and cluster labels remain aligned.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists introspection engine, mongod, REST status, and collection sizing arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, and tstats appear; final table lists twenty-one analyst-facing columns including app_context, criticality, and owner_hint.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.7 Splunk KV Store health: fuse _introspection sourcetype=kvstore engine and collection sizing, _internal sourcetype=mongod WiredTiger style signals, REST /services/kvstore/status replication snapshot, and join inventory plus tstats volume proxy. Tunables: earliest=-4h@h cache_hit_warn=0.82 oplog_page_sec=120 oplog_warn_sec=45 eviction_page=8000 conn_warn=7000 growth_warn_gib_per_hr=4")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_introspection sourcetype=kvstore earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,shcluster_label,shc_id,"standalone"))))
      | eval cache_hit_ratio=tonumber(coalesce(cache_hit_ratio,wiredTiger_cache_hit_ratio,cache_hit_pct,"0"))
      | eval dirty_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(dirty_bytes,wt_dirty_bytes,bytes_dirty,"0"))
      | eval eviction_pressure=tonumber(coalesce(eviction_pressure,wt_eviction_pressure,evictions_per_sec,"0"))
      | eval connections_active=tonumber(coalesce(connections_active,current_connections,mongo_connections_current,"0"))
      | eval oplog_lag_seconds=tonumber(coalesce(oplog_lag_seconds,repl_lag_sec,replication_lag_sec,"0"))
      | eval replSetMembers=coalesce(replSetMembers,member_health_summary,replication_member_state,"")
      | eval kvstore_collection=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(collection,kvstore_collection,table,metric_scope,"engine_rollups"))))
      | eval db_size_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(db_size_bytes,size_bytes,storageSize,dataSize,"0"))
      | eval replication_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(replication_status,kv_replication_status,"intro_lane"))))
      | eval lane="introspection_kvstore_engine"
      | fields _time host cluster lane cache_hit_ratio dirty_bytes eviction_pressure connections_active oplog_lag_seconds replSetMembers kvstore_collection db_size_bytes replication_status ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=mongod earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,shcluster_label,shc_id,"standalone"))))
      | eval cache_hit_ratio=tonumber(coalesce(cache_hit_ratio,cache_ratio,"0"))
      | eval dirty_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(dirty_bytes,bytesCurrentlyInTheCacheDirty,tracked_dirty_bytes,"0"))
      | eval eviction_pressure=tonumber(coalesce(eviction_pressure,eviction_server_walksPerSec,walks_per_sec,"0"))
      | eval connections_active=tonumber(coalesce(connections_active,connections_current,conn_current,"0"))
      | eval oplog_lag_seconds=tonumber(coalesce(oplog_lag_seconds,optime_lag_seconds,secondary_lag_seconds,"0"))
      | eval replSetMembers=coalesce(replSetMembers,repl_member_state,memberState,"mongod_log")
      | eval kvstore_collection="mongod_diag"
      | eval db_size_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(db_size_bytes,dataSizeBytes,"0"))
      | eval replication_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(replication_status,"mongod_internal"))))
      | eval lane="internal_mongod"
      | fields _time host cluster lane cache_hit_ratio dirty_bytes eviction_pressure connections_active oplog_lag_seconds replSetMembers kvstore_collection db_size_bytes replication_status ]
    [ | rest splunk_server=local /services/kvstore/status count=0
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,serverName,servername,"rest_local"))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(shcluster_label,shc_id,"rest_status"))))
      | eval replication_status=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(replication_status,status,title,"rest_unknown"))))
      | eval replSetMembers=coalesce(replSetMembers,members,member_status,content_replSetMembers,"rest_members_unparsed")
      | eval oplog_lag_seconds=tonumber(coalesce(oplog_lag_seconds,optimeLag,optime_lag_seconds,"0"))
      | eval cache_hit_ratio=0
      | eval dirty_bytes=0
      | eval eviction_pressure=0
      | eval connections_active=0
      | eval kvstore_collection="mongo_replset_status"
      | eval db_size_bytes=0
      | eval lane="rest_kvstore_status"
      | fields _time host cluster lane replication_status replSetMembers oplog_lag_seconds cache_hit_ratio dirty_bytes eviction_pressure connections_active kvstore_collection db_size_bytes ]
    [ search index=_introspection sourcetype=kvstore earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,shcluster_label,shc_id,"standalone"))))
      | eval kvstore_collection=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(collection,kvstore_collection,app_collection,ns_collection,"unscoped_collection"))))
      | eval db_size_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(db_size_bytes,size_bytes,storageSize,allocated_bytes,"0"))
      | where db_size_bytes>1048576 OR match(kvstore_collection,"notable_audit|incident_review|service_kpi|itsi|lookup")
      | eval cache_hit_ratio=0
      | eval dirty_bytes=0
      | eval eviction_pressure=0
      | eval connections_active=0
      | eval oplog_lag_seconds=0
      | eval replSetMembers="size_only"
      | eval replication_status="collection_metrics"
      | eval lane="kvstore_collection_sizes"
      | fields _time host cluster lane kvstore_collection db_size_bytes cache_hit_ratio dirty_bytes eviction_pressure connections_active oplog_lag_seconds replSetMembers replication_status ]
| eval cluster=coalesce(cluster,"unknown_cluster")
| eval host=coalesce(host,"unknown_host")
| eval kvstore_collection=coalesce(kvstore_collection,"unspecified_kv_collection")
| sort 0 + cluster + host + kvstore_collection - _time
| streamstats window=8 current=t global=f last(db_size_bytes) AS prev_db_bytes last(_time) AS prev_ts BY cluster host kvstore_collection
| eval growth_bytes_per_hr=if(isnotnull(prev_db_bytes) AND isnotnull(prev_ts) AND (_time-prev_ts)>120, round((db_size_bytes-prev_db_bytes)/((_time-prev_ts)/3600.0),4), null())
| eventstats max(eval(if(lane=="rest_kvstore_status",oplog_lag_seconds,null()))) AS cluster_peak_oplog max(eval(if(match(kvstore_collection,"notable_audit|incident_review|service_kpi"),db_size_bytes,null()))) AS cluster_sensitive_coll_peak BY cluster
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen
    max(cache_hit_ratio) AS cache_hit_ratio
    max(dirty_bytes) AS dirty_bytes
    max(eviction_pressure) AS eviction_pressure
    max(connections_active) AS connections_active
    max(oplog_lag_seconds) AS oplog_lag_seconds
    max(db_size_bytes) AS db_size_bytes
    max(growth_bytes_per_hr) AS growth_bytes_per_hr
    values(replSetMembers) AS replSetMembers
    values(lane) AS lane_vals
    values(replication_status) AS repl_status_vals
    first(host) AS rep_host
  BY cluster kvstore_collection
| eval coalesce_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lane_vals),0)
| eval repl_status=mvindex(mvdedup(repl_status_vals),0)
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(oplog_lag_seconds,0)>=120 OR match(lower(mvjoin(replSetMembers," ")),"down|rollback|unknown|invalid"),"page",
    coalesce(oplog_lag_seconds,0)>=45,"warn",
    coalesce(cache_hit_ratio,0)>0 AND coalesce(cache_hit_ratio,1)<0.82 AND coalesce(coalesce_lane,"x")=="introspection_kvstore_engine","warn",
    coalesce(eviction_pressure,0)>=8000,"page",
    coalesce(connections_active,0)>=7000,"warn",
    coalesce(growth_bytes_per_hr,0)>=4294967296,"warn",
    match(lower(coalesce(repl_status,"")),"degrad|recover|initialsync|rollback"),"page",
    true(),"info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    coalesce(oplog_lag_seconds,0)>=45,"validate_shc_kv_primary_secondary_order_and_disk_latency_then_mc_kv_dashboard",
    coalesce(cache_hit_ratio,0)>0 AND coalesce(cache_hit_ratio,1)<0.82,"increase_wiredtiger_cache_or_reduce_kv_write_load_and_disk_contention",
    coalesce(eviction_pressure,0)>=8000,"relieve_wiredtiger_eviction_pressure_scale_disk_or_reduce_bulk_outputlookup",
    coalesce(connections_active,0)>=7000,"find_connection_storms_from_correlation_searches_and_stuck_scheduled_lookups",
    coalesce(growth_bytes_per_hr,0)>=4294967296,"trim_notable_audit_incident_review_service_kpi_collections_after_backup",
    true(),"refresh_kvstore_status_rest_and_compare_mc_replication_panels")
| join type=left max=0 kvstore_collection
    [| inputlookup splunk_kvstore_collection_inventory.csv
     | eval kvstore_collection=lower(trim(toString(kvstore_collection)))
     | fields kvstore_collection app_context criticality owner_hint es_itsi_signal ]
| join type=left max=0 rep_host
    [| tstats count WHERE index=_introspection sourcetype=kvstore earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY splunk_server
     | eval rep_host=lower(trim(toString(splunk_server)))
     | stats sum(count) AS kv_introspection_evt_volume BY rep_host ]
| fillnull value=0 kv_introspection_evt_volume
| eval app_context=coalesce(app_context,"unmapped_collection")
| eval criticality=coalesce(criticality,"medium")
| eval owner_hint=coalesce(owner_hint,"platform_kv_ops")
| eval es_itsi_signal=coalesce(es_itsi_signal,"none")
| table cluster rep_host kvstore_collection cache_hit_ratio dirty_bytes eviction_pressure connections_active oplog_lag_seconds replSetMembers db_size_bytes growth_bytes_per_hr severity recommended_action last_seen coalesce_lane repl_status app_context criticality kv_introspection_evt_volume es_itsi_signal owner_hint
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS host
| eval host=lower(trim(host))
| table host cpu_load mem_used
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by cluster and kvstore_collection with drilldowns to _introspection kvstore JSON, mongod lines, REST status rows, inventory metadata, and Monitoring Console KV panels; sparklines for growth_bytes_per_hr and oplog_lag_seconds; single-value tiles for cluster_peak_oplog.

## Known False Positives

Approved maintenance that rolls KV members in sequence will temporarily raise oplog_lag_seconds and flip replication verbs until secondaries catch up; require change windows and UC-13.1.10 captain stability context before paging rule authors. Nightly backup or resync jobs can inflate db_size_bytes and IOPS without user-visible errors; compare spikes to backup schedules and snapshot tooling. Lab clusters that import production bundles for testing may show huge notable_audit or incident_review footprints that are expected in non-production; use inventory criticality tags to downgrade routing. Sparse mongod sourcetype adoption on some hosts can make the internal_mongod arm quiet even when introspection is healthy; validate forwarding policy instead of muting the alert. REST /services/kvstore/status from search heads in strict lockdown may be blocked while introspection still flows from peers; prefer indexed REST pollers or service accounts rather than silencing replication logic. Version upgrades sometimes rename introspection keys until coalesce ladders are refreshed; expect short false warn bursts after upgrades if parsers lag documentation. Penetration or vulnerability scanners that hammer management ports can elevate connections_active without genuine lookup storms; pair with job inspector before blaming correlation searches. Duplicate forwarders indexing the same introspection stream can skew growth math until dedupe summaries land. Executive dashboards that only watch replication_status green may miss eviction_pressure warnings that still merit tuning during batch outputlookup seasons.
